sanskrit:
तानि सर्वाणि संयम्य युक्त आसीत मत्परः।
वशे हि यस्येन्द्रियाणि तस्य प्रज्ञा प्रतिष्ठिता॥
transliteration: tāni sarvāṇi saṃyamya yukta āsīta mat-paraḥ / vaśe hi yasyendriyāṇi tasya prajñā pratiṣṭhitā
literal_meaning: Having restrained all those (senses), the integrated person should sit with Me as the supreme goal; for one whose senses are under control, his wisdom is firmly established.
contextual_meaning: Lasting emotional regulation is impossible without a trans-personal anchor; when attention is lovingly yoked to a benevolent higher presence, the nervous system calms and clarity becomes stable.
psychological_state: Secure attachment to an internalized compassionate figure; prefrontal cortex online, amygdala quiet.
trigger_scenario: You feel your anger surge after a Twitter pile-on; instead of doom-scrolling you close your eyes, recall a memory of your grandmother’s loving presence, breathe slowly, and the urge to retaliate dissolves.
anchor_text: Rest your mind in the calm presence that holds all thoughts like the sky holds clouds.
